title: SRPT Stock Plunges After-Hours As Elevidys Demand Slumps - CEO Still Sees Return To Growth
text:
Shares of Sarepta Therapeutics fell 7% in after-hours trading following the company's Q1 2026 earnings release, despite beating Wall Street expectations on both revenue and earnings. The stock plunge reflected investor concerns about declining demand for the company's flagship gene therapy ELEVIDYS.

Earnings Beat vs. Stock Reaction: Sarepta reported earnings per share of $3.16, far surpassing the forecast of $0.99. Revenue reached $731 million against a forecast of $474.26 million. Despite this strong beat, the market focused on ELEVIDYS weakness rather than the positive earnings surprise.

ELEVIDYS Demand Softness: Net product revenue dropped significantly to $330.5 million in Q1 2026 from $611.5 million in Q1 2025. ELEVIDYS net product revenue: $102.0 million, down from higher levels in prior year. The decline was attributed to updated label restrictions and market hesitancy.

PMO Franchise Strength: PMO franchise revenues reached $228.6 million, exceeding ELEVIDYS for the first time in recent quarters. This demonstrated resilience of the legacy RNA-based therapies despite gene therapy focus.

CEO Commentary: Despite the stock plunge, CEO Doug Ingram indicated confidence in ELEVIDYS returning to growth. Management attributed the demand softness to label restrictions and worked on regulatory solutions through the ENDEAVOR Cohort 8 trial with sirolimus pretreatment.

Market Sentiment: The mixed reaction highlights how investors balance earnings beats against concerns about the commercial viability and market acceptance of the flagship product. Gene therapy market concerns and competitive dynamics were also weighing on sentiment.
